name: Punctuation & Formatting (English)
description: Punctuation, capitalization, and formatting accuracy for English

tests.groundTruth: Lets eat grandma
tests.formattedGroundTruth: Let's eat, Grandma!

tests.groundTruth: A woman without her man is nothing
tests.formattedGroundTruth: A woman: without her, man is nothing.

tests.groundTruth: Stop clubbing baby seals
tests.formattedGroundTruth: Stop clubbing, baby seals!

tests.groundTruth: I like cooking my family and my pets
tests.formattedGroundTruth: I like cooking, my family, and my pets.

tests.groundTruth: Most of the time travelers worry about their luggage
tests.formattedGroundTruth: Most of the time, travelers worry about their luggage.

tests.groundTruth: The White House released a statement on Tuesday
tests.formattedGroundTruth: The White House released a statement on Tuesday.

tests.groundTruth: Apple announced the new iPhone at their annual event
tests.formattedGroundTruth: Apple announced the new iPhone at their annual event.

tests.groundTruth: Professor Smith works at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology
tests.formattedGroundTruth: Professor Smith works at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology.

tests.groundTruth: The United Nations held a summit in New York City
tests.formattedGroundTruth: The United Nations held a summit in New York City.

tests.groundTruth: World War Two ended in nineteen forty-five
tests.formattedGroundTruth: World War Two ended in 1945.

tests.groundTruth: When is the meeting At three o'clock
tests.formattedGroundTruth: When is the meeting? At three o'clock.

tests.groundTruth: Have you finished the report Yes since yesterday
tests.formattedGroundTruth: Have you finished the report? Yes, since yesterday.

tests.groundTruth: That is amazing When can we start
tests.formattedGroundTruth: That is amazing! When can we start?

tests.groundTruth: Why isn't this working Have you checked the logs
tests.formattedGroundTruth: Why isn't this working? Have you checked the logs?

tests.groundTruth: Incredible I never would have thought that
tests.formattedGroundTruth: Incredible! I never would have thought that.

tests.groundTruth: The results see table three are conclusive
tests.formattedGroundTruth: The results (see table 3) are conclusive.

tests.groundTruth: We have known each other for years said both of them
tests.formattedGroundTruth: "We have known each other for years," said both of them.

tests.groundTruth: There are three options first wait second act third cancel
tests.formattedGroundTruth: There are three options: first, wait; second, act; third, cancel.

tests.groundTruth: The price for example fifty dollars is negotiable
tests.formattedGroundTruth: The price - for example, fifty dollars - is negotiable.

tests.groundTruth: New York not to be confused with New York City
tests.formattedGroundTruth: New York (not to be confused with New York City).

tests.groundTruth: The day was long. First we had a meeting. Then came the presentation. In the end everyone was satisfied
tests.formattedGroundTruth: The day was long. First, we had a meeting. Then came the presentation. In the end, everyone was satisfied.

tests.groundTruth: Good morning. How are you today. I hope you had a nice weekend
tests.formattedGroundTruth: Good morning. How are you today? I hope you had a nice weekend.

tests.groundTruth: The project has three phases. Phase one is planning. Phase two is execution. Phase three is testing
tests.formattedGroundTruth: The project has three phases. Phase one is planning. Phase two is execution. Phase three is testing.

tests.groundTruth: Please note the following points. First arrive on time. Second bring your documents
tests.formattedGroundTruth: Please note the following points. First: arrive on time. Second: bring your documents.

tests.groundTruth: The sun is shining. It is warm outside. A perfect day for a walk
tests.formattedGroundTruth: The sun is shining. It is warm outside. A perfect day for a walk.
